title: padus racemosa f. commutata
intro: Padus racemosa f. commutata, also known as the Himalayan cherry or black cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Himalayan region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, offering health benefits such as immune system support and relief from respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves, bark, and fruits of Padus racemosa f. commutata can be used to make teas, infusions, and tonics, which can be consumed for their medicinal properties.
